info.title: Ferretly External API
info.description: The Ferretly External API allows integration partners to create and manage subjects, initiate background screenings, and retrieve screening results programmatically.

get.summary: Get information about the API client associated with the API key found in the request header
get.description: Using the API key found in the request header, return information about the API client

post.summary: Update the web hook url associated with the API client. This is the URL which will receive callbacks from Ferretly

get.summary: Refresh the API key associated with an API client.
Get the Ferretly Data.DTO.Organization related to the API key

post.summary: Update the Data.Models.ExternalAPI.ExternalAPIClientEntity.ClientSpecificProperties associated with the API client.

requestBody.description: The new value to store as the Data.Models.ExternalAPI.ExternalAPIClientEntity.ClientSpecificProperties

get.summary: Gets the preference profiles for the authenticated client's organization.
Get a list of all of the subscriber's subjects

400.description: Requested Analyst Support but organization does not have Analyst Support

get.summary: Get a particular subject by external id value
get.description: If there are multiple subjects having the specified externalId value, the "first" will be returned

get.summary: Get status of background screen for the requested subject
Get status of background screen for the requested subject

parameters.name: externalId
parameters.description: The "external" id value for the subject. This value is specified by the API client when the subject is created.

get.summary: Get results from most recently-completed background screen for the requested subject
Get results from most recently-completed background screen for the requested subject

400.description: Something was invalid about the request: subject does not have a completed background check; subject currently has background check in-progress

get.summary: Initiate the social media screening portion of a background check for the specified subject. If your organization has Analyst Assistance, it's likely that you should not be using this endpoint. :)

400.description: Subject does not exist or has no social media accounts associated with it, is not valid for the API client, already has a background check in progress, or organization does not have enough credits.

200.description: Successfully attempted to start background check

get.summary: Get filtered list of posts for the specified subject

400.description: Subject does not exist, is not valid for the API client.

get.summary: Get filtered list of posts for the specified subject

400.description: Subject does not exist, is not valid for the API client.

get.summary: Download the latest background report for the requested subject

400.description: The subject does not have a current background report

404.description: The subject has a current background report, but it could not be located in Blob Storage

profileUrl.description: The direct URL to the profile on the social media platform.

isPersonalProfile.description: Indicates whether this handle represents a personal profile, as opposed to a business or organization page.

riskMakeups.description: Risk makeups: count of posts flagged for each risk

searchIteration.description: Represents a search iteration step where this candidate came from. Null for non-search (validation) candidates

socialMediaProfileId.description: Represents a unique candidate id provided by platform

isNotAvailableForHandle.description:
A Boolean indicating whether this profile is not available for use as
a subject handle. This is typically used for profiles that are
found to be duplicates or otherwise unsuitable for handles.

PricingModel.description: Represents the pricing model for a product

ProfileIdentifiers.description: A ProfileIdentifier represents the data that was provided in the profile

SearchPlatform.description: Enumeration of the various searchable platforms

isPersonalProfile.description: Indicates whether this handle represents a personal profile, as opposed to a business or organization page.

ApiKey.description: API key authentication. Pass your API key in the X-Api-Key request header.
ApiKey.name: X-Api-Key

tags.name: ApiClient
tags.description: Endpoints for discovering information about and managing Data.Models.ExternalAPI.ApiClient instances.

tags.name: PreferenceProfiles
tags.description:
The PreferenceProfilesController provides endpoints to manage the preference profiles
related to an authenticated client.

tags.name: Subjects
tags.description: Endpoints for creating, discovering, and managing Ferretly subject records associated with an API client
